node.title: Atlassian P5 | Uber L5a | Rippling L7 | Vmware SMTS

topic.title: Atlassian P5 | Uber L5a | Rippling L7 | Vmware SMTS

post.content:
Summary : 9 Years experience in non-FAANG, top products companies.
Prepared from March 2022 to June 2022

Leetcode 300+ Total (50Hard+200Medium+50Easy), not all in this period

Focused the preparation more on System design in last 2 months as I was getting rejected there in previous interviews. That could be one reason of not doing very well in lc heavy Google interviews in later half.
I had interviewed with 10 companies and got rejected in first 4 and got offers from last 6.

**Rejected** : Skyflow , Confluent, Rubrik, UiPath (All senior engineer kind of roles)
**Offered** : Atlassian P5, Uber L5a, Rippling L7, Vmware SMTS, Eightfold Lead/Senior, Google (offered L4, interviewed for L5)

Difficulty order 

Confluent ~= Uber > Rubrik > VMware ~= Atlassian > Google > Eightfold

TC wise
Atlassian > Uber > Vmware > Rippling (excluding paper esops), declined Google, Eightfold without discussing on numbers
 
**Atlassian** | Medium Difficulty | Remote (Bangalore)
1. Karat screen with 2 dsa and a system design rapid fire. Cleared as I had previously did similar thing for indeed/coinbase.
2. DSA+LLD : dsa question all over leetcode, just need to take care of clean coding practices.
3. DSA : again all over internet, suggested 2 approaches, written clean & modular code for 1 of the approach.
4.  HLD : Not over leetcode and not the common one but I did well from requirements to desiging the whole system.
5.  Values : Similar to other posts covering 5-6 company values
6.  Management round : similar to other experiences covering examples from past experience

**Uber L5a** | Difficulty Hard | Bangalore
1. DSA : popular leetcode medium on graph but slightly changed to hard if you were to space optimize the graph from matrix to map, clean code & completion of both the matrix & map approach within 1 hour was enough for interview to give me a hire call.
2. DSA+LLD : Never seen before on leetcode posts, written interface and 2 classes for 2 use cases. I could write only 1 but correctly, still got a hire without being able to comlete both the use cases.
3. System Design : Not so popular design question but managed to complete the given use cases within time with a nice diagram. 
4. Bar raiser (Past design) : Past technical project explanatin and tech learnings, challanges etc
5. Hiring Manager : Best hiring manager round with examples from past experience for typing behavioural scenarios

**TC Offered : ~130L (approx, ceilling)**

**Rippling L7** | Difficulty Medium-Hard | Bangalore
1. DSA : Never solved before but medium difficulty leetcode question
2. DSA : others have posted similar question, not on lc
3. Design-1 : other have posted similar to that
4. Design-2 : again similar to other posts on lc
5. HM : easiest, few basic question on current work experience + product demo. I asked a lot of questions on tech behind and it turned me off

Decided not to join Rippling because of their WLB, inflated valuation & lower quality engg work so gave them a very high expectation numbers, recruiter never came back (as expected).

**Vmware SMTS** | Difficulty Medium | Bangalore
1.  Initial screening : With Director, discussion on current work & what they are looking for, basic technical questions
2.  DSA [1 hour] : Digging on past work exp and then solved 2 questions medium lc
3. LLD+Coding [1 hour ] : role was golang+kubernetes specific, so provided the lld & written code in golang.
4. DSA [1 hour] : 1 LC medium on binary tree, provided clean code, ran it. 

**TC Offered : ~100L** with very low RSUs, I was most interested to join this but declined due to broadcom stuff.

**Google L5** | Difficulty medium | Bangalore
1. DSA : medium difficult question, explained & written clean code. interviewer gave feedback with nitpicks & red flagged the revisions which i made while initial approach vs implementation, obviously you will uncover things as you go, but looks like they want you to mug up solutions and vomit out perfectly.
2. DSA : medium difficulty, non lc, explained and solved just fine, again got nitpick feedback
3. DSA : lc hard, explained & code covered most execpt an edge case, revised the approach but couldn't fix code due to strict time.
4. Design : Very domain specific design question, covered most aspects of design and got nitpicked feedback.

Asked if I am interested in L4, declined without a second thought, heard downlevel stories on blind but it was first hand experience and Google interview feedbacks were a big time turn off. Looks like if you mug-up code+time/space complexities and give answers without thinking/revising, you are good, otherwise you struggled to do this/that. Obviously you are not supposed to evaluate your approaches this vs that while you code, example writing a separate utility function vs putting something in complex if condition, I got a negative nitpick for this one.

**Eightfold Senior/Lead Engineer** | Medium difficulty
1. LLD : Gave some vauge robot problem, had to design class intereaction and their functions
2. HLD : Domain specific question, solved it and gave some lld and hld. The interviewer was some 3 year exp guy with not so good communication skills (no ego issues for sure over exp). I had to litterly explain him that this/that features is provided by Kafka which I used in my hld. This round turned me off with the quality of their engineering team.
3. HM : Past work, what I a looking for in new job etc.
Declined to enggage as they said they can't match my previous offers with all cash. I was not interested to hold paper esops over public RSUs from others.

**Confluent Senior Engineer** | Hard difficulty level (Rejected)
Similar to what others have posted. 
1. DSA  : DP Hard 
2. Concurrency coding : popular concurrency interview question
3. Design-1 : similar to twitter/insta feed generation
4. Design-2 : popular hld question from gr...ing sys-de88gn
I got hire except deisgn-1 round for minor things and was rejected. I had to improve a lot on concurrency problems for Confluent and they helped me later on in other interviews.

**Rubrik Senior Engineer** | Medium-Hard (Rejected)
1. Concurrency Coding : popular concurrency question
2. DSA : popular design+dsa question using dll & map
3. Design-1 : similar to other posts
4. Design-2 : design of one of the linkedin feature
5. HM : past experience


**UiPath Senior Engineer** | Medium (Rejected)
1. DSA : sliding window based popular question
2. DSA : never saw on lc, gave a wrong greedy solution, which looked to be working for many test cases and interviewer could give single example to break the wrong solution, realized after interview.
3. Design (LLD) : lld, class diagram of something like stackoverflow. 
Rejected after this.

**Skyflow Senior Engineer** | Medium-Hard (Rejected)
1. DSA : something on binary tree, solved
2. DSA : never seen something like this on LC but solved & written the code 
3. DSA+Design : Graph Hard, solved using backtracking + design of hit counter (something like a website views with multiple instances)

For help other than interview question details reach : soft.sidecar@gmail.com
If people cant share the actual questions, whats the point of sharing dsa1 dsa2\n\nWe all know uber asks dsa..
Congrats ! , I have also given the atlassian interview and you can watch my experience on youtube : https://youtu.be/uEJMIrtnJng
Congrats! Could you please share your profile and years of experience and also the timelines of these interview processes?
How did you prepare for system design interviews?\nEven i struggle there
For google bit I agree with you. Had faced the same issue. Looks like they are looking for robots
Congrats! how many years of experience do you have? Quality > Quantity, still how many lc problems did you solve?
What\'s the point of sharing this post if u cant share even a single question.
what was the concurrency question in confluent?
Hey mate have some questions to.clarify, have sent you email. Hope you are still active

Can you share the offer numbers ? I have same level of experience and preparing for same companies which you have mentioned (Atlassian , Google , Uber , Rubrik ) . raushankiit1992@gmail.com. Thanks in Advance
Congrats! Could you please share the Atlassian HLD round question ?
For lld and dsa, which language did you use?
Congratulations! Just curious to know which one are you planning to join out of Uber and Atlassian?
Congratulations. Could you tell me how did you prepare for LC/SD/OOD along with a job?\n
Hey, congratulations for the offers. Very well deserved. Since you don\'t want to share the numbers publicly here, let\'s get in touch via email? (ptghustler@gmail.com)
